assertions: After `/z-eval-update`, `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` and `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` are read and `readManifestSnapshot()` is consulted before rediscovery begins.
Rediscovery output only mentions targets whose on-disk primitives differ from the manifest snapshot according to `config.update.criticalChangeRules`.
Dry-run mode performs no `askQuestion` prompts and emits no accepted filesystem patches from regeneration helpers.
The emitted summary names the dry-run mode and refrains from reporting fresh `git_ref`, `updated_at`, or `manifest.history.yml` append activity tied to apply-only step 6.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases walk every `/z-eval-update` invocation style from rediscovery through targeted apply, the CI parity gate, cache-only refreshes, and the hard failures when required eval-system files are absent. Assertions stay tied to externally visible transcripts, exit codes, manifest touches, and the documented preservation guards for user-owned eval rows and tests.

cases.expected_output: The run loads the eval config, reads the live manifest plus the snapshot helper, rediscovers targets from `discovery_config`, classifies drift with the critical-change rules, and prints a drift-oriented summary without writing regenerated tests or appending manifest history from an apply path.

assertions: After `/z-eval-update --apply`, a `zoto-eval-updater` subagent runs using the `zoto-update-evals` skill.
Each time the subagent returns `needs_user_input`, the command issues `askQuestion`, then resumes with the operator choice until the loop completes.

Unless `--no-analyser` is set, every added or modified target triggers `runAnalyser` with invalidate true before dispatching to `regeneratePytest`, `regenerateVitest`, `regenerateJest`, `regenerateLlmCode`, or `regenerateLlmDeclarative` as configured.

Helpers refuse to overwrite tests unless `isGeneratedFile` passes and refuse to mutate `evals.json` rows unless `isGeneratedCase` passes, leaving user-authored rows lacking `_meta.generated === true` and user-authored tests missing the first-line `_meta.generated` marker untouched.
When `--no-analyser` is omitted, missing `CURSOR_API_KEY` prevents a successful remote analyser refresh and blocks writes until credentials are supplied or the operator switches modes.

Accepted updates rewrite `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` with a new `git_ref`, `updated_at`, and `targets[]`, append `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml`, and close with `{ mode, regenerated_targets, files_written, files_preserved_user_authored, user_cases_preserved, reports[] }`.

cases.expected_output: The updater subagent drives per-target regeneration, the host issues one `askQuestion` per presented diff and resumes with each answer, helpers stamp only generated cases or files, and accepted work refreshes the manifest plus history while reporting preserved user-owned artifacts.

No `askQuestion` calls occur in this mode.
The process exits with `config.update.checkExitCodeOnCriticalDrift` (default 2) on critical drift and 0 otherwise, matching `pnpm run eval:update -- --check` for automation hooks.

assertions: When `process.env.CI` equals `true`, stderr after `/z-eval-update --check --no-analyser` includes `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser used in CI; cached analyser payloads may be stale and produce drift`.

No `zoto-eval-updater` subagent starts and no drift regeneration runs in this failure path.
